mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a robust labor market and expanding economy, which directly supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward returns for S&P 500 show no statistically significant positive correlation with PAYEMS growth over a 5-year rolling window, or the Rank IC is consistently below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPIAUCSL) shows a sustained acceleration, it signals rising inflation expectations, prompting the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, which increases discount rates and negatively impacts S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 40% of instances where the CPIAUCSL trigger is met, or the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong consumer confidence and investment, signaling broad economic expansion that boosts corporate revenues and drives S&P 500 returns higher over a one-month period.
failure_condition: The joint condition of strong RSAFS and HOUST growth does not lead to positive S&P 500 20-day returns in at least 65% of triggered instances over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) from a low base indicates a deteriorating labor market and potential economic contraction, leading to a sharp decline in corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 returns over a short-term horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points in 3 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 30% of instances where the UNRATE trigger is met, or the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive momentum in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects robust manufacturing activity and supply-side strength, which underpins corporate profitability and supports positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The 60-day forward returns for S&P 500 show no statistically significant positive correlation with INDPRO growth over a 5-year rolling window, or the Rank IC is consistently below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) crosses a specific threshold, it signals a restrictive monetary policy stance, increasing the cost of capital and reducing liquidity, which negatively impacts equity valuations and S&P 500 returns in the very short term.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 35% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 1.5%, or the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP (GDPC1) combined with strong retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust aggregate demand and consumer spending, which provides a strong fundamental backdrop for corporate earnings growth and S&P 500 performance over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 2-quarter annualized growth > 3.0% AND RSAFS 3-month growth > 1.5%
failure_condition: The joint condition of strong GDPC1 and RSAFS growth does not lead to positive S&P 500 60-day returns in at least 60% of triggered instances over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of consistent job creation (PAYEMS) and a stable or declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) signifies a healthy and expanding labor market, which underpins consumer confidence and spending, leading to positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth > 100k AND UNRATE 3-month change <= 0.1 percentage points
failure_condition: The combined signal of strong PAYEMS growth and stable UNRATE does not result in positive S&P 500 5-day returns in at least 70% of triggered instances over a 7-year backtest.
